summary: Completed comprehensive testing of CrediControl SaaS loan management system after MongoDB to Supabase PostgreSQL migration. Backend APIs 100% working (21/21 tests passed). Frontend fully functional with all pages loading correctly and data displaying properly.
passed_tests: API root endpoint returns version info (Supabase 2.0.0)
Admin login (emersonthiago257@gmail.com / 123456) - works correctly
User login (teste2@teste.com / 123456) - works correctly
Auth cookies (httpOnly, secure, samesite=none) set correctly
Auth /me endpoint returns user data with valid session
Logout clears session and redirects to login
Admin dashboard shows stats (2 users, 4 loans, R$ 22.100,00)
Admin users list shows 2 users with actions (view, edit, block, delete)
Admin access denied for regular users (403)
User dashboard shows financial metrics (R$ 14.000,00 loaned, 3 clients, 3 loans)
Customers list endpoint works
Customer CRUD (create, read, delete) works with UUID IDs
Loans list shows 3 loans with customer names
Installments list shows 15 installments with filters
Installments stats (11 pending, 1 overdue, 3 paid)
Installments filter by status works
Settings page shows user profile and preferences
Preferences endpoint works
Role-based access control enforced (user blocked from admin, admin blocked from user routes)
Navigation between all pages works correctly
Pay installment button visible and functional

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all pages load, navigation works, data displays correctly)

seed_data_creation: Data migrated from MongoDB to Supabase PostgreSQL (3 users, 5 clients, 4 loans, 18 installments, 5 payments)
context_for_next_testing_agent: MongoDB to Supabase migration completed successfully. All backend APIs use supabase-py async client with service_role key. IDs are now UUIDs. Frontend unchanged - works with string IDs. All CRUD operations verified working. Auth uses HTTP-only cookies with CORS configured for HTTPS.
